content.problemStatement: Q4) A . Differentiate between structure and union ?   ---3 Marks.
constraints: Each word consists of printable ASCII characters only
content.outputFormat:
Print the following three lines exactly as shown, each on its own line:
Structure: each member has its own memory location; size is sum of members (with padding).
Union: all members share the same memory location; size is size of largest member.
Access: structure members can be used simultaneously; union members cannot be used simultaneously.

sampleTestCases.output:
Structure: each member has its own memory location; size is sum of members (with padding).
Union: all members share the same memory location; size is size of largest member.
Access: structure members can be used simultaneously; union members cannot be used simultaneously.
sampleTestCases.explanation: The program prints the three required lines describing the differences.

sampleTestCases.output:
Structure: each member has its own memory location; size is sum of members (with padding).
Union: all members share the same memory location; size is size of largest member.
Access: structure members can be used simultaneously; union members cannot be used simultaneously.
sampleTestCases.explanation: Even with no input, the output remains the same fixed description.

sampleTestCases.output:
Structure: each member has its own memory location; size is sum of members (with padding).
Union: all members share the same memory location; size is size of largest member.
Access: structure members can be used simultaneously; union members cannot be used simultaneously.
sampleTestCases.explanation: The problem does not depend on input; the output is constant.

solution.approach: The problem does not require any computation. The required answer is a fixed description of the differences between a structure and a union. The solution simply prints the three lines in the given order. No input is read, and the program terminates after printing.
